edges.description: Reduced T2 activity blocks terminal isoleucine catabolic cleavage.
Reduced T2 activity impairs ketolysis and ketone body handling in extrahepatic tissues.
Blocked terminal isoleucine catabolism causes buildup of 2-methylacetoacetate, 2-methyl-3-hydroxybutyrate, tiglylglycine, and related acylcarnitines.
Impaired ketone body utilization predisposes to recurrent ketoacidotic crises under catabolic stress.
2-methyl-3-hydroxybutyrate accumulates in urine as a characteristic isoleucine-derived marker.
Tiglylglycine accumulates in urine as a characteristic isoleucine-derived marker.
2-methylacetoacetate can be excreted in large amounts during acute crisis.
Tiglyl-CoA accumulation is reflected by elevated C5:1 tiglylcarnitine in blood acylcarnitine profiles.
2-methyl-3-hydroxybutyryl-CoA accumulation is reflected by elevated C5-OH acylcarnitine.
Accumulated isoleucine-derived metabolites may contribute to neurologic injury through a crisis-independent neurotoxicity route.
edges.target: Neurological complications and basal ganglia involvement

edges.description: Organic acid accumulation contributes to acidotic metabolic crises when catabolic stress increases pathway flux.
intermediate_mechanisms: Accumulated organic acids contribute to high-anion-gap acidemia during catabolic stress.

edges.description: Decompensation episodes manifest as recurrent ketoacidosis.
Severe crises include metabolic acidosis.
Severe ketoacidotic crises produce an acute syndrome with toxic encephalopathy, gastrointestinal symptoms, abnormal tone, and compensatory tachypnea.
intermediate_mechanisms: Severe acidosis and toxic metabolite burden impair brain function during crisis.

edges.description: Acute illness and impaired energy handling can be accompanied by stress hyperglycemia or hypoglycemia.
Severe acidotic decompensation can progress to respiratory failure requiring intensive support.
intermediate_mechanisms: Severe acidosis and altered consciousness can compromise ventilation in acute crisis.

edges.description: Decompensation episodes can leave neurologic sequelae, while some neurologic complications may also appear without an obvious crisis.
edges.target: Neurological complications and basal ganglia involvement

edges.description: Toxic encephalopathy during crisis reduces arousal and activity.
Vomiting occurs during the acute ketoacidotic crisis syndrome.
Hypotonia can occur as part of the acute ketoacidotic crisis syndrome.
Tachypnea accompanies the acute ketoacidotic crisis syndrome and acidotic compensation.
Severe encephalopathic crises can include tonic-clonic seizures.
Severe toxic encephalopathy can progress to coma.
Acute illness can produce short-lived stress hyperglycemia during BKD crises.
Some biochemical crisis presentations occur with hypoglycemia.
Basal ganglia involvement can manifest as bilateral basal ganglia lesions.
edges.source: Neurological complications and basal ganglia involvement

edges.description: Neurologic involvement can include psychomotor developmental delay.
edges.source: Neurological complications and basal ganglia involvement

edges.description: Increased urinary 2-methyl-3-hydroxybutyrate reports the accumulated isoleucine-derived organic acid pool.
Elevated 2-methyl-3-hydroxybutyrate reflects blocked terminal isoleucine catabolism.
Increased urinary tiglylglycine reports the accumulated isoleucine-derived organic acid pool.
Elevated tiglylglycine reflects blocked isoleucine catabolic flux through the T2-dependent step.
Increased urinary 2-methylacetoacetate reports the accumulated isoleucine-derived organic acid pool during crisis.
Elevated 2-methylacetoacetate reflects failure to clear the T2 substrate branch of isoleucine catabolism.
Elevated C5:1 tiglylcarnitine reports acylcarnitine conjugation of accumulated isoleucine-derived intermediates.
Elevated C5:1 tracks blocked isoleucine catabolic flux.
Elevated C5-OH reports acylcarnitine conjugation of accumulated 2-methyl-3-hydroxybutyryl intermediates.
Elevated 2-methyl-3-hydroxybutyrylcarnitine tracks blocked isoleucine catabolic flux.

nodes.description: Severe ketoacidotic episodes can present with toxic metabolic encephalopathy and associated acute-crisis signs, including reduced arousal, vomiting, hypotonia, tachypnea, seizures, and coma.

evidence.reference_title: A Novel Mutation of Beta-ketothiolase Deficiency: The First Report from Iran and Review of Literature.
evidence.snippet: clinical signs and symptoms of toxic encephalopathy such as lethargy, hypotonia, vomiting, tachypnea, and coma in some patients

nodes.description: Compound heterozygosity for two different ACAT1 pathogenic variants is a common genotype. For example, ACAT1 c.481T>C, p.(Tyr161His) was identified in compound heterozygosity in an adult patient presenting with diabetic ketoacidosis.

evidence.reference_title: Diabetic ketoacidosis in an adult with beta-ketothiolase deficiency (BKD) involving a novel ACAT1 variant : first report of established diabetes in BKD and a review of the literature.
evidence.snippet: genetically confirmed compound heterozygosity for variants in ACAT1, including a novel ACAT1 c.481T>C, p.(Tyr161His) variant.

nodes.description: Recurrent episodes of ketoacidosis are the hallmark manifestation. Episodes are triggered by catabolic stressors such as infection, fasting, or ketogenic diet, typically presenting in infancy or early childhood with high anion gap metabolic acidosis and ketonuria.

evidence.reference_title: Diabetic ketoacidosis in an adult with beta-ketothiolase deficiency (BKD) involving a novel ACAT1 variant : first report of established diabetes in BKD and a review of the literature.
evidence.snippet: BKD typically manifests in childhood as recurrent episodes of ketoacidosis, the frequency of which tends to reduce with age.

nodes.description: Under catabolic triggers such as infection, prolonged fasting, or ketogenic diet, increased reliance on ketone body flux and amino acid catabolism overwhelms the blocked T2 pathway. Accumulated organic acids produce high anion gap metabolic acidosis with ketosis and variable glycemia. Between crises, biochemical profiles may normalize, complicating detection. Episodes decrease in frequency with age, and are rare after age 10 in most patients.

evidence.reference_title: Diabetic ketoacidosis in an adult with beta-ketothiolase deficiency (BKD) involving a novel ACAT1 variant : first report of established diabetes in BKD and a review of the literature.
evidence.snippet: BKD typically manifests in childhood as recurrent episodes of ketoacidosis, the frequency of which tends to reduce with age.

evidence.reference_title: Molecular characterization, clinical phenotype, and neurological outcome of twelve Palestinian children with beta-ketothiolase deficiency: report of two novel variants in the ACAT1 gene.
evidence.snippet: Ketoacidotic episodes were the predominant manifestations in all patients, and each episode was triggered by either acute gastroenteritis or upper respiratory infections.

nodes.description: Founder mutations have been identified in specific populations. In a Palestinian cohort, a founder mutation was found in six patients from three families. All affected patients were offspring of consanguineous marriages, increasing the likelihood of homozygous pathogenic variants.

evidence.reference_title: Molecular characterization, clinical phenotype, and neurological outcome of twelve Palestinian children with beta-ketothiolase deficiency: report of two novel variants in the ACAT1 gene.
evidence.snippet: a founder mutation was identified in six patients from three families.

nodes.description: Stress hyperglycemia is not uncommon during acute ketoacidotic crises in children with BKD. Persistent dysglycemia with elevated HbA1c has been reported in an adult patient, suggesting potential coexisting diabetes.

evidence.reference_title: Diabetic ketoacidosis in an adult with beta-ketothiolase deficiency (BKD) involving a novel ACAT1 variant : first report of established diabetes in BKD and a review of the literature.
evidence.snippet: Stress hyperglycemia is not uncommon when children with BKD are acutely unwell, with several pediatric case reports describing short-lived hyperglycemia but normal HbA1c measurements during metabolic crises

nodes.description: Hypoglycemia may occur during metabolic crises, reflecting impaired energy metabolism and variable glycemic responses.

evidence.reference_title: Molecular characterization, clinical phenotype, and neurological outcome of twelve Palestinian children with beta-ketothiolase deficiency: report of two novel variants in the ACAT1 gene.
evidence.snippet: T2 deficiency is characterized by the accumulation of isoleucine metabolites, 2methylacetoacetate, 2-methyl-3-hydroxybutyrate, and tiglylglycine, detected in urine organic acids and blood acylcarnitines with or without hypoglycemia.

nodes.description: ACAT1/T2 catalyzes the thiolytic cleavage of 2-methyl-acetoacetyl-CoA into propionyl-CoA and acetyl-CoA, the terminal step in the isoleucine degradation pathway. Loss of T2 activity blocks this step, leading to accumulation of upstream isoleucine-derived metabolites including 2-methyl-3-hydroxybutyrate, 2-methylacetoacetate, and tiglylglycine.

evidence.reference_title: Diabetic ketoacidosis in an adult with beta-ketothiolase deficiency (BKD) involving a novel ACAT1 variant : first report of established diabetes in BKD and a review of the literature.
evidence.snippet: Beta-ketothiolase deficiency (BKD) is a rare autosomal recessive condition affecting isoleucine catabolism and ketone body metabolism.

evidence.reference_title: Mutation update on ACAT1 variants associated with mitochondrial acetoacetyl-CoA thiolase (T2) deficiency.
evidence.snippet: Mitochondrial acetoacetyl-CoA thiolase (T2, encoded by the ACAT1 gene) deficiency is an inherited disorder of ketone body and isoleucine metabolism.

nodes.description: T2 is involved in ketogenesis in the liver (synthesizing acetoacetyl-CoA from acetyl-CoA) and in the terminal step of ketolysis in extrahepatic tissues (cleaving acetoacetyl-CoA to two acetyl-CoAs). Loss of T2 activity impairs extrahepatic ketone body utilization, predisposing to ketosis and ketoacidosis under catabolic stress.

evidence.reference_title: Diabetic ketoacidosis in an adult with beta-ketothiolase deficiency (BKD) involving a novel ACAT1 variant : first report of established diabetes in BKD and a review of the literature.
evidence.snippet: BKD typically manifests in childhood as recurrent episodes of ketoacidosis, the frequency of which tends to reduce with age.

evidence.reference_title: Mutation update on ACAT1 variants associated with mitochondrial acetoacetyl-CoA thiolase (T2) deficiency.
evidence.snippet: Mitochondrial acetoacetyl-CoA thiolase (T2, encoded by the ACAT1 gene) deficiency is an inherited disorder of ketone body and isoleucine metabolism.

nodes.description: Failure to clear 2-methylacetoacetyl-CoA redirects upstream isoleucine intermediates into diagnostic organic acids and acylcarnitines, including 2-methyl-3-hydroxybutyrate, tiglylglycine, 2-methylacetoacetate, tiglylcarnitine, and 2-methyl-3-hydroxybutyrylcarnitine.

evidence.reference_title: Molecular characterization, clinical phenotype, and neurological outcome of twelve Palestinian children with beta-ketothiolase deficiency: report of two novel variants in the ACAT1 gene.
evidence.snippet: T2 deficiency is characterized by the accumulation of isoleucine metabolites, 2methylacetoacetate, 2-methyl-3-hydroxybutyrate, and tiglylglycine, detected in urine organic acids and blood acylcarnitines with or without hypoglycemia.

evidence.reference_title: Metabolic encephalopathy in beta-ketothiolase deficiency: the first report from India.
evidence.snippet: During the acute crisis the C5OH (2-methyl-3-hydroxybutyryl) carnitine and C5:1 (tiglyl) carnitine were elevated and large amounts of 2-methyl-3-hydroxybutyrate, tiglylglycine, and 2-methylacetoacetate were excreted.

nodes.description: Almost all of the 56 disease-associated missense ACAT1 variants affect residues that are completely or partially buried in the T2 structure, causing deficiency through lower in vivo T2 activity due to reduced folding efficiency or stability.

evidence.reference_title: Mutation update on ACAT1 variants associated with mitochondrial acetoacetyl-CoA thiolase (T2) deficiency.
evidence.snippet: Almost all these missense variants concern residues that are completely or partially buried in the T2 structure. Such variants are expected to cause T2 deficiency by having lower in vivo T2 activity because of lower folding efficiency and/or stability.

nodes.description: Hypotonia is observed during acute metabolic crises and may be a presenting feature in some patients even before the first recognized ketoacidotic episode.
